title: Add Multi-Digit Numbers by Place Value
description: Add two multi-digit numbers within 1,000 by decomposing into hundreds, tens, and ones and combining partial sums.
condition: Two 2- or 3-digit numbers to be added (within 1,000).
response: Decompose into hundreds, tens, ones; add like place values; combine sums.
label: Principle
notes: Includes composing a new ten or hundred when needed.

title: Add Using Compensation Strategy
description: Adjust one addend to a friendly number and compensate to find the correct sum.
condition: Addition problem where one addend is close to a friendly number (e.g., 19 close to 20).
response: Add using adjusted number, then compensate to correct the total.
label: Principle

title: Arrange Digits to Optimize Sum or Difference
description: Use place-value reasoning to arrange given digits into two numbers to maximize or minimize sum or difference.
condition: Four given digits to form two two-digit numbers without repetition; goal to maximize/minimize sum or difference.
response: Assign larger digits to tens place (for larger values) or balance digits to minimize difference; compute result.
label: Principle
notes: Requires reasoning about place value and optimization.

title: Compare Measurement Units by Size
description: Reason that larger units require fewer counts and smaller units require more counts for the same length.
condition: Same object measured in two different units (e.g., centimeters vs feet).
response: Determine which unit yields a greater numerical measure and select true statements.
label: Principle
example: A hallway measured in centimeters has more units than when measured in feet because centimeters are smaller.
notes: Inverse relationship between unit size and count.

title: Compare Numbers by Place Value
description: Compare two numbers within 1,000 using place value to determine >, <, or =.
condition: Two numbers (standard or expanded form) to compare.
response: Compare hundreds, then tens, then ones; select correct comparison symbol.
label: Principle
notes: May require first evaluating expanded form.

title: Compose or Decompose Tens/Hundreds
description: Regroup ones into a ten or tens into a hundred (or reverse) during addition or subtraction.
condition: Place-value operation where a column sums to 10 or more, or subtraction requires borrowing.
response: Compose a new ten/hundred or decompose a higher unit.
label: Principle

title: Evaluate Expanded Form
description: Convert a number written in expanded form into standard form by summing place-value parts.
condition: Number written as a sum of hundreds, tens, and ones.
response: Add the parts to determine the standard-form number.
label: Concept
notes: Response is constant for a given expression.

title: Find Difference Between Lengths
description: Measure or use given lengths and subtract to find how much longer one is than another.
condition: Two lengths given or measured in same units.
response: Subtract shorter length from longer length.
label: Principle
example: If lengths are $15$ cm and $8$ cm, then $15-8=7$ cm.

title: Interpret Line Plot Data
description: Read a line plot to count data points at or above a value and determine differences.
condition: Line plot with Xs marking data values.
response: Count relevant marks or compute difference between extreme values.
label: Principle
example: If longest chain is $22$ in. and shortest is $13$ in., difference is $22-13=9$.
notes: Includes counting frequencies and comparing values.

title: Skip-Count by Tens
description: Identify numbers that occur when counting by 10s from a given starting point.
condition: Counting by 10 from 0 (or multiple of 10) within a range.
response: Recognize or state numbers that are multiples of 10 in the sequence.
label: Principle

title: Subtract Multi-Digit Numbers by Place Value
description: Subtract two multi-digit numbers within 1,000 using place-value reasoning and regrouping if needed.
response: Subtract hundreds, tens, ones; regroup if necessary.
label: Principle
notes: May use number line or decomposition.

title: Subtract Using Compensation
description: Subtract a nearby friendly number and adjust to maintain equality.
condition: Subtraction where subtrahend is close to a friendly number (e.g., 596 close to 600).
response: Subtract friendly number, then compensate by adding or subtracting the difference.
label: Principle
